notes: One mistral-vibe session and one claudecode session driven concurrently in the SAME cwd by run-cell-multi.sh, observed by a single --record daemon. The daemon labels each adapter independently (disjoint ProcessMatchers + transcript roots: ~/.vibe/logs/session vs ~/.claude/projects; maps.go keys everything by adapter Identity.Name), so the workspace events.jsonl carries two distinct sessions — one per adapter — each with its own working->ready arc and no cross-contamination. This spec is adapter-agnostic: agentA is whichever session enters working first, agentB the other; both cells (mistral-vibe + claudecode) share this expected.jsonl and the same workspace events.jsonl (each cell differs only in which transcript it stores for the replay golden). Every phase anchors on a WORKING transition, never a ready, because a born-ready proc-<PID> presession row may precede a real session (claudecode always; vibe unconfirmed from source) — anchoring on working skips those placeholders, and new_session on agentB skips agentA's already-matched session. Asserts: two distinct sessions, each an independent working->ready arc, in one workspace. BACKFLOW (re-spec): mistral-vibe is a presession adapter — birth re-templated as presession_birth->session_birth(new_session) from the codex sibling (a transient proc-<PID> row is born ready before the real session_<id> transcript dir appears, then reconciles under a DIFFERENT session_id); every post-birth phase pins same_session_as session_birth. OBSERVATIONS: the offline replay golden cannot reproduce vibe's meta.json sidecar (model + tokens are sidecar-only, read live), so model and tokens are UNVERIFIABLE offline — like kiro-cli the observation vector asserts neither model nor tokens (agent only, cost_nonzero=false, tokens_nonzero=false). SCOPE: this vibe cell asserts ONLY the mistral-vibe session's own presession->session_birth->working->ready arc; the concurrently-driven claudecode session in the shared workspace events.jsonl is out of scope for the vibe spec (each adapter is labeled independently by disjoint ProcessMatchers + transcript roots). KNOWN_FAILING (GitHub issue prepared; see #905/#906 siblings): recorded 2026-07-08-08-59-01 verifies 3/5 phases — turn_start/turn_end FAIL because session_birth's next-new_session matcher has no adapter/session-id filter and binds the concurrent claudecode partner's own presession-birth (born ready ~0.9s after vibe's presession, inside the 30s window) instead of vibe's real session (born ready ~13.8s later). The daemon behaves correctly — this is a spec-matcher gap, not a daemon bug; see the sibling commit ebc58f06 for the original honest VALIDATION DRIFT capture. A correct fix needs a same-adapter (or same-session-prefix) filter capability in the matcher grammar, not a rebase of this expected.jsonl. Drop this flag once that capability exists.
source: .specs/agent-scenarios.md → Feature: Multiple agents in same workspace

text: proc-<PID> pre-session appears in ready within 1s of launch (PID bound before the transcript dir exists)
Real session_<id> reconciles (different session_id) when the transcript directory appears, born ready
vibe PID bound to the real session (matched by cwd)
The vibe session's turn drives ready -> working on the real session (same cwd as the concurrent claudecode session)
The vibe session's turn completes: working -> ready, labeled independently with no cross-contamination
